questions.stem: This colonic finding developed after broad-spectrum penicillin therapy. Which organism is responsible?

explanation.summary: Antibiotic-associated pseudomembranous colitis results from overgrowth of toxin-producing C. difficile spores after normal flora is disrupted; the yellow-white plaques are the pseudomembranes.
explanation.whyCorrect: Antibiotic-associated pseudomembranous colitis results from overgrowth of toxin-producing C. difficile spores after normal flora is disrupted; the yellow-white plaques are the pseudomembranes.

questions.stem: A patient given aminopenicillin develops this widespread maculopapular eruption. Concurrent infection with which condition classically precipitates it?

explanation.summary: Ampicillin or amoxicillin given during EBV mononucleosis produces a characteristic non-allergic maculopapular rash; it does not indicate a true penicillin allergy.
explanation.whyCorrect: Ampicillin or amoxicillin given during EBV mononucleosis produces a characteristic non-allergic maculopapular rash; it does not indicate a true penicillin allergy.

questions.stem: The highlighted four-membered ring in this core antibiotic structure allows the drug to act as a structural analogue of which bacterial cell-wall substrate?

explanation.summary: The strained beta-lactam ring mimics the D-Ala-D-Ala terminus of the peptidoglycan pentapeptide, letting penicillin covalently bind and irreversibly inhibit transpeptidase (PBP).
explanation.whyCorrect: The strained beta-lactam ring mimics the D-Ala-D-Ala terminus of the peptidoglycan pentapeptide, letting penicillin covalently bind and irreversibly inhibit transpeptidase (PBP).

questions.stem: These four illustrated panels summarize the principal adverse-effect categories of which antibiotic class?

explanation.summary: Hypersensitivity reactions, diarrhea/C. difficile colitis, interstitial nephritis, and hematologic toxicities are the classic adverse effects associated with penicillin-class beta-lactam therapy.
explanation.whyCorrect: Hypersensitivity reactions, diarrhea/C. difficile colitis, interstitial nephritis, and hematologic toxicities are the classic adverse effects associated with penicillin-class beta-lactam therapy.
